title: [lichen_l2.c:507-543] SECURITY: Constant-time iteration leaks via lichen_link_rx timing
description:
Security finding: The constant-time peer iteration (lines 507-543) is undermined by lichen_link_rx() itself.

Code analysis:
- Lines 494-500 document intent: 'Always iterate through ALL peers even after finding a match'
- But lichen_link_rx() (lines 517-528) performs crypto operations with variable timing:
  - SCHC decompression (data-dependent)
  - AES-CCM verification (fixed time, good)
  - Schnorr-48 verify (depends on pubkey/signature values)

Attack scenario:
1. Attacker sends frames and measures response time
2. Even with all peers checked, lichen_link_rx() timing varies
3. The FIRST successful verification adds SCHC decompression time
4. Subsequent iterations call lichen_link_rx() with wrong pubkeys (faster fail)
5. Total time varies based on WHICH peer index matched

Note: The existing issue project-LICHEN-i1gk.61 mentions weak constant-time claim but doesn't detail the lichen_link_rx() timing leak.

Impact: P2 - requires precise timing measurement, but could reveal peer ordering.

Mitigation:
1. Parse frame ONCE before signature loop (already decompressed doesn't help)
2. Use constant-time comparison in Schnorr-48 verify
3. Or accept timing leak as low-risk for constrained devices
close_reason: Documented: architecture limitation - weak constant-time due to variable timing in SCHC decompression and Schnorr-48 verify. Accepted risk: attack requires sub-millisecond timing precision over LoRa which is impractical.
